pageTitle: All cities with concrete flatwork cost data | Woogoro
metaDescription: Concrete flatwork cost data by city and state. Cubic-yard delivered cost, rebar spec, finish, and pump access drive per-square-foot pricing more than slab area.
ogTitle: Concrete flatwork cost by city | Woogoro
ogDescription: Concrete flatwork pricing by city. Cubic-yard cost, rebar inclusion, finish, and pump-vs-wheelbarrow access by metro.
schemaDescription: Directory of U.S. cities with 2026 concrete flatwork cost data, organized by state, including delivered cost and finish-mix context per metro.
intro: Concrete flatwork pricing moves on cubic-yard delivered cost, slab thickness, rebar specification, and finish (broom vs stamped vs polished). Freeze-thaw zones (Upper Midwest, New England, Mountain West) require 4–6% air entrainment that adds $8–$15 per yard at the plant. Stamped or colored finishes layer a 60–90% labor premium over straight broom finish. The single biggest regional swing is concrete pumping vs wheelbarrow placement: dense urban driveways with no truck access in Boston and San Francisco run $400–$800 in pump time that doesn't show up on a Phoenix lot at all. Each linked metro page lists the local cubic-yard delivered cost, rebar-inclusion norm, finish mix, and the pump-vs-wheelbarrow access pattern that drives quotes there.
